Emne: Emne: Sundhed i Danmark med fokus på kost og motion
Praesentation:
Hej. I dag vil jeg gerne tale om sundhed i Danmark. Jeg vil især fokusere på kost og motion, fordi det betyder meget for danskernes hverdag.

Først vil jeg tale om kost. Mange danskere går meget op i at spise sundt. De officielle kostråd i Danmark siger, at vi skal spise flere grøntsager, mere fisk og mindre kød. Alligevel er det ikke alle, der følger rådene. I en travl hverdag køber mange mennesker færdigretter, som ofte indeholder for meget sukker og fedt. Desuden er fastfood blevet meget populært blandt de unge.

Dernæst vil vi se på motion. Generelt er danskerne et meget aktivt folkefærd. Rigtig mange cykler til arbejde eller skole, hvilket er en god form for hverdagsmotion. Derudover er det populært at gå i fitnesscenter eller gå ture i naturen. Men der er også en udfordring. Mange voksne har et stillesiddende arbejde, hvor de sidder foran en computer hele dagen. Det kan føre til rygproblemer og livsstilssygdomme.

Tilsidst vil jeg sige, at Danmark gør meget for sundheden, men der er stadig plads til forbedring. Det er vigtigt, at staten gør det billigere at købe sunde madvarer, så alle har råd til at leve sundt.

Tak fordi I lyttede.
Subject: Topic: Health in Denmark with a focus on diet and exercise
Presentation:
Hello. Today I would like to talk about health in Denmark. I would especially like to focus on diet and exercise, because it is very important for Danes' everyday lives.

First, I would like to talk about diet. Many Danes are very keen on eating healthily. The official dietary guidelines in Denmark say that we should eat more vegetables, more fish and less meat. However, not everyone follows the advice. In a busy everyday life, many people buy ready-made meals, which often contain too much sugar and fat. In addition, fast food has become very popular among young people.

Next, we will look at exercise. In general, Danes are a very active people. Many people cycle to work or school, which is a good form of everyday exercise. In addition, going to the gym or going for walks in nature is popular. But there is also a challenge. Many adults have a sedentary job where they sit in front of a computer all day. This can lead to back problems and lifestyle diseases.

Finally, I would like to say that Denmark does a lot for health, but there is still room for improvement. It is important that the government makes it cheaper to buy healthy food so that everyone can afford to live a healthy life.

Thank you for listening.
